note: Start the runtime-ownership run by proving current drop-in wire behavior still matches pinned Valkey. This is the compatibility floor for every later perf/architecture packet.
Capture the current pipeline-depth matrix before any runtime-ownership edits. Numbers are evidence, not a claim of parity.
Short stack-sampling run to confirm the post-0004595 hotspot shape before architecture packets fire. Uses smoke-size workloads so the loop can make progress unattended.

Architect-only. Do NOT implement the runtime rewrite. Read latest benchmark/profile evidence, map the faithful Valkey runtime ownership boundary, refine this packet graph if needed, and explicitly mark sharding / benchmark-only bypasses as non-goals. Any implementation packets you create must preserve normal dispatch, ACL, MULTI/EXEC, scripting, expiration, pub/sub, blocking wakeups, AOF, replication, and RDB semantics.

Add a wire-diff corpus script that stresses the semantics most likely to regress under runtime ownership: transaction order, WATCH invalidation, expiration-on-access, pub/sub non-interference if expressible in the corpus, blocking-command edge cases if safe/non-hanging, selected DB behavior, and script/replication-safe command ordering where supported. Do not edit existing corpus files or normalize away differences.
Run the full wire-diff corpus after adding runtime-owner canaries. If this fails, fix behavior or canary scope before any runtime scaffold work.

Create a typed RuntimeOwner scaffold without changing the default product path. Allowed: new runtime_owner module, inert types such as RuntimeOwner, ClientSlot, RuntimeEvent, RuntimeOwnerConfig, tests proving construction/queue semantics. Allowed in main.rs only: module declaration or clearly disabled wiring. Forbidden: benchmark-only GET/SET/PING/INCR path, bypassing dispatch, disabling TLS/pubsub/blocking/replication/AOF/RDB, or making the scaffold default before oracle evidence exists.
Compatibility gate after the inert runtime-owner scaffold. The scaffold is only acceptable if normal Valkey behavior remains byte-compatible on the surveyed corpus.
Refresh the profile matrix after the scaffold. Expected result may be neutral; this packet confirms no accidental slowdown and gives the next architect packet real evidence.
Longer sampled run after the safe scaffold. This is the evidence handoff for the next human/architect decision: proceed to real owner-loop migration, take a small allocator/hasher cleanup, or stop.
